ankous_shadow.name: Ankou's Shadow
description: ## Ankou's Shadow
‹SOURCE Legacy of the First World/14›

Ankous are terrifying, winged fey assassins dispatched by the greatest lords of the First World to eliminate rivals. Some mortals emulate ankous to become the perfect assassins; others believe themselves to be ankous incarnated in mortal shells on a deep cover mission. In either case, an ankou's shadow practices the deadliest skills of the First World's most feared enforcers.

::ab[Shadow Double (Sp)]{icon=power fullround="An ankou's shadow can create a single, quasi-real, shadowy duplicate. This *shadow double* remains in his square, mimicking his movements as a single ‹spell/mirror image›, except that it lasts until it is destroyed or he chooses to dismiss it as a @HLswift_action. This ability does not stack with the *mirror image* spell or with similar abilities, such as the *copycat* ability of the ‹domain/Trickery› domain." imp5="An ankou's shadow gains a second *shadow double.* In addition to using these *shadow doubles* as *mirror images* in his square, he can move his doubles as part of his own move action, dividing his movement between himself and his doubles. When outside his square, *shadow doubles* do not protect the ankou's shadow as *mirror image* and are limited in the actions they can take. Shadow doubles provide ‹rule/flanking› for the ankou's shadow and his allies, but they do not possess teamwork feats or special abilities that alter the effects of flanking or aiding another. As a @HLswift_action, the ankou's shadow can direct his *shadow doubles* to use the ‹rule/aid another<_2› action, using his own base attack bonus plus his Intelligence modifier for the roll. Although a *shadow double* appears to duplicate the ankou's shadow's gear, this gear is part of its form; a *shadow double's* gear cannot be destroyed, dropped, or stolen. A *shadow double* disappears if it ventures more than 50 feet from the ankou's shadow or if it leaves his line of sight or effect. A *shadow double* that is hit by an attack roll or takes any damage is destroyed. The AC of a *shadow double* is equal to the ankou's shadow's touch AC, and it has the same CMD and saving throw bonuses as the ankou's shadow. Shadow doubles possess ‹ability/evasion› if the ankou's shadow does. Mind-affecting effects targeting a *shadow double* affect the ankou's shadow instead, though he isn't affected twice by effects that target both him and a *shadow double.*" imp10="An ankou's shadow gains a third *shadow double.* He can divide his actions between his actual body and his *shadow doubles,* using them as the origin point for attacks or abilities. For example, an ankou's shadow making three attacks as a part of a full attack could make his primary attack from his own body and his other two attacks from two of his *shadow doubles.*" imp15="An ankou's shadow gains a fourth *shadow double.* In addition to the abilities above, an ankou's shadow can assign the doubles to perform simple tasks on their own, as the ‹spell/unseen servant› spell, except that the *shadow doubles* have an effective Strength score of 10." replace="Studied target"}

::ab[Ankou's Vision (Sp)]{l=7 icon=magic swift="An ankou's shadow can use ‹spell/see invisibility›, as per the spell, for 1 minute per level per day. This duration does not need to be consecutive, but it must be used in 1-minute increments." replace=Stalker}

::ab[Shadow Prey (Ex)]{icon=down-info info="An ankou's shadow cannot denote a target as his *quarry* unless he has a *shadow double* present, but unlike other slayers, the target does not need to be one of his *studied targets.*" alter="Quarry~improved quarry"}

::ab[Unfettered Shadows (Su)]{l=20 icon=power standard="An ankou's shadow can unfetter all of his *shadow doubles* for 1 minute. While unfettered, each *shadow double* can move and attack as if it were the ankou's shadow without using any of his actions. A creature struck by a *shadow double* can attempt a Will save (DC 20 + the ankou's shadow's Intelligence modifier) each time it takes damage to recognize the illusion. If it succeeds, the target treats all the *shadow doubles* as 20% real, as the spell ‹spell/shadow conjuration›." special="The *shadow doubles* cannot use the *shadow double* ability." useMod=Intelligence3 useUnit=time}

::list[The following slayer talents complement the ankou's shadow archetype]{hl link=slayertalent all="rogue talent|‹talent/bleeding attack›, ‹talent/fast stealth›, ‹talent/surprise attack›~slowing strike"}
::list[The following advanced slayer talents complement the ankou's shadow archetype]{hl link=slayertalent all="advanced rogue talent|hunter's surprise~assassinate~woodland stride"}

avalancher.name: Avalancher
description: ## Avalancher
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/86›
Avalanchers attack from overhead cliffs and mesas like a landslide of arrows and blades.

::ab[Falling Strike (Ex)]{icon=boost flavor="An avalancher excels at dropping onto opponents from higher ground." passive="He gains a bonus on Stealth checks and attack and damage rolls when he falls at least 10 feet before attacking an opponent. These bonuses are equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fifth› of his slayer level, and they stack with any bonuses the avalancher receives for ‹rule/charging› or attacking from ‹rule/higher ground›." replace="Studied target"}

::ab[Cliff Jumper (Ex)]{icon=boost-def order=pa passive="An avalancher adds half his level on Acrobatics checks to jump or soften a fall." ability="Whenever he attempts an ‹skill/Acrobatics› check to soften an intentional fall, he reduces the falling damage he takes by an additional 1d6 for every increment of 10 by which his Acrobatics check result exceeds the DC (a 2d6 reduction at DC 25, 3d6 at DC 35, and so on)." imp11="The avalancher reduces the DC of his Acrobatics checks to soften a fall by 5." replace="Track~swift tracker"}

::ab[Falling Dodge (Ex)]{l=7 icon=def ability="An avalancher gains his bonus to AC from the *falling strike* ability when a jump or fall carries him through threatened squares." special="This bonus applies only when he's jumping, not when he's using Acrobatics to try to avoid attacks of opportunity from moving through threatened squares." replace=Stalker}

::ab[Fall-By Attack (Ex)]{l=14 icon=power ability="If the avalancher makes a single jump of at least 20 feet in any direction and lands at an elevation at least 10 feet lower than his starting elevation, he can make ranged or melee attacks during his fall as if he were making a full-round attack. He can make any number of attacks in any combination of squares along this single jump, up to his maximum number of attacks, but he can attack a given creature once at most. If the avalancher attempts a jump but fails the Acrobatics check, he makes no attacks and suffers the consequences of his failed jump normally." imp19="He can add his extra damage from *sneak attack* against each creature he hits while using this ability, even if the targets have acted in combat or retain their Dexterity bonuses to AC." replace="Quarry~improved quarry"}

::ab[Death from Above (Ex)]{l=20 icon=power flavor="An avalancher can take down his foe with a single attack." ability="During a round, if he falls at least 10 feet (the action type depends on how he comes to be falling), he can make a single attack at his full base attack bonus as a @HLstandard_action, choosing one of the following effects: kill, knock unconscious for 1d4 hours, or paralyze for 2d6 rounds. If the attack hits, the target takes damage normally and must succeed at a Fortitude save or suffer the additional effect. The save DC is equal to 20 + 1 for every 10 feet the avalancher fell before making the attack (to a maximum of DC 30 for a fall of 100 feet or more)." special="Whether or not the target succeeds, it can't be targeted by this ability again (by any avalancher) for 24 hours." replace="Master slayer"}

bloody_jake.name: Bloody Jake
description: ## Bloody Jake
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/66›
Bloody jakes are cruel backwoods folk who prey upon their country cousins while terrorizing civilized people who venture into their rural range.
::ab[Alignment]{icon=warning prereq="A bloody jake must be of an evil alignment." info="A bloody jake who becomes nonevil cannot gain new levels as a slayer but retains all his class abilities."}

::ab[Favored Terrain (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost choice="A bloody jake can select a type of terrain from the ‹ability/favored terrain› table for ‹class/ranger«s›, though he is restricted to cold, desert, forest, jungle, mountains, plains, and swamp." imp5="His bonuses in the selected terrain increase by 2." incrementPlain="His bonuses in the selected terrain now increase~5~5~4~2" incrementDesc=by special="He does not gain the ability to select additional *favored terrains* unless he takes the ‹talent/terrain mastery› rogue talent." replace="Proficiency in medium armor and shields"}

::ab[Cruel Tricks]{head icon=power ability="Bloody jakes use backwoods folk magic to confuse, terrify, and weaken their prey. He gains the *cruel tricks* below at the indicated slayer levels." useLMod=slayer~Charisma replace="The 1st-level studied target~slayer talents gained at 4th, 8th, 12th, and 16th level"}

::ab[Distant Voices (Sp)]{next l=1 icon=magic ability="A bloody jake can cause his voice to seem to emanate from somewhere else, as per ‹spell/ventriloquism›. This ability doesn't take any other action to activate and can be used as part of speaking (as a @HLfree_action); it costs one use of *cruel tricks* to affect his speech for 1 round."}

::ab[Lose Your Way (Sp)]{next l=4 icon=lower standard="A bloody jake can spend one use of *cruel tricks* to cause a creature that is within 30 feet and within the bloody jake's *favored terrain* to become disoriented, taking a penalty on Survival checks equal to the bloody jake's slayer level and a -2 penalty on saving throws against fear and illusions. This effect lasts for 1 hour per slayer level of the bloody jake or for as long as the affected creature remains within the bloody jake's *favored terrain,* whichever is shorter."}

::ab[Something's Not Right (Su)]{next l=8 icon=lower move="A bloody jake can spend one use of *cruel tricks* to cause all foes within 30 feet to become ‹misc/shaken› for 1 round. This is a fear effect that doesn't increase in strength by applying other effects that cause creatures to become shaken." imp12="He can use this ability as a @HLswift_action." imp16="A bloody jake can make any number of the affected foes ‹misc/frightened› for 1 round, though such foes receive a Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the loody jake's slayer level + his Charisma modifier) to negate the frightened condition; even so, such a foe is still shaken."}

::ab[The Woods Have Eyes (Sp)]{next l=12 icon=power swift="A bloody jake in his *favored terrain* can spend one use of *cruel tricks* to gain ‹umr/blindsense› in a 30-foot radius for 1 round; this blindsense is sound-based."}

::ab[Poor Study (Ex)]{l=5 icon=down ability="A bloody jake gains *studied target* at 5th level rather than 1st level, with a bonus equal to ‹misc/one-fifth› of his slayer level." imp11="A bloody jake may study an opponent as a @HLmove or @HLswift_action." alter="Studied target"}

::ab[Sadistic Snare (Ex)]{l=6 icon=power ability="A bloody jake gains ‹feat/Learn Ranger Trap› as a bonus feat, benefiting from it as if he were a ranger. He can expend two uses of his traps ability to add his sneak attack damage to the trap's damage; any slayer talents or rogue talents he has that modify sneak attack damage, such as ‹talent/bleeding attack›, also apply to sneak attack damage dealt by his ranger traps." replace="Slayer talent gained at 6th level"}

::ab[Cruel Trick (Ex)]{l=11 icon=lower ability="A bloody jake can maim or humiliate his target with a sneak attack, substituting 1 point of ‹rule/Dexterity or »Charisma damage› per die of sneak attack damage forfeited. A successful Will save negates this ability damage (DC = 10 + 1/2 the bloody jake's slayer level + his Charisma modifier)." replace="Swift tracker"}

::ab[Woodland Shortcut (Sp)]{l=13 icon=magic ability="A bloody jake can move with supernatural ease among the trees once per day, as if using ‹spell/tree stride› with a caster level equal to his slayer level." replace="Slayer's advance"}

bounty_hunter.name: Bounty Hunter
description: ## Bounty Hunter
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/118›
Whether tasked with bringing in wanted criminals or paid to drag debtors back to their loan sharks, bounty hunters are valued for their ability to capture targets alive.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power-down info="A bounty hunter is proficient with all simple and martial weapons plus the ‹eq-weapon/aklys›, ‹eq-weapon/bolas›, ‹eq-weapon/dan bong›, ‹eq-weapon/lasso›, and ‹eq-weapon/net›. Bounty hunters are proficient with light armor, light shields, and bucklers, but not heavy shields or tower shields." replace="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Dirty Trick (Ex)]{l=2 icon=lower ability="Anytime a bounty hunter is able to deal sneak attack damage to a *studied target,* he can instead attempt to hamper the target. The bounty hunter must declare that he's using this ability before the attack roll is made. If the attack hits, it deals damage normally, but instead of rolling sneak attack damage, the bounty hunter can attempt a ‹rule/dirty trick› combat maneuver against the *studied target* as a free action, adding 1 to the combat maneuver check for each die of the bounty hunter's sneak attack damage. This combat maneuver does not provoke attacks of opportunity." replace="Slayer talent gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Submission Hold (Ex)]{l=6 icon=melee ability="When a bounty hunter attempts a ‹rule/grapple› combat maneuver check to deal damage, he can choose to add his sneak attack damage to the grapple damage for that attack. He takes a -5 penalty on the combat maneuver check if he does this. This sneak attack damage is nonlethal damage, unless the bounty hunter is able to deal normal damage with a grapple and chooses to do so." replace="Slayer talent gained at 6th level"}

::ab[Incapacitate (Ex)]{l=10 icon=lower ability="A bounty hunter can incapacitate a *studied target.* This functions like the ‹slayertalent/assassinate› slayer talent, except instead of killing the target, the bounty hunter's successful attack knocks the target unconscious for 1d6 rounds unless it succeeds at its saving throw. If the target does succeed at its saving throw, it still takes the sneak attack damage as normal, but the damage is nonlethal, and the target is immune to that slayer's incapacitate ability for 24 hours." replace="The advanced slayer talent gained at 10th level"}

::list[The following slayer talents complement the bounty hunter archetype]{hl link=slayertalent all="rogue talent|‹talent/camouflage›, ‹talent/fast stealth›, ‹talent/hard to fool›, ‹talent/snap shot›, ‹talent/surprise attack›, ‹talent/terrain mastery›~slowing strike"}

::list[The following advanced slayer talents complement the bounty hunter archetype]{hl link=slayertalent all="advanced rogue talent|‹talent/hunter's surprise›, ‹talent/knock-out blow›, ‹talent/master of disguise›~woodland stride"}

butterfly_blade.name: Butterfly Blade
description: ## Butterfly Blade
‹SOURCE Heroes of the High Court/24›

Followers of a tradition dating back to the ancient empire of Yixing, the yellow-robed agents known as butterfly blades now operate primarily in Goka, where many serve the city's politically savvy ruler. Butterfly blades work in the shadows, eliminating threats with their namesake weapons and intimidating troublesome nobles.

::ab[Studied Stalker (Ex)]{icon=power-down ability="A butterfly blade gains the *stalker* class feature at 1st level, rather than at 7th level." special="The butterfly blade doesn't add his *studied target* bonus on Knowledge or Survival checks." alter="Studied target" replace=Track}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power choice="Whenever a butterfly blade would gain a slayer talent, he can instead select a feat from the following list: ‹feat/Cutting Humiliation›, ‹feat/Enforcer›, ‹feat/Intimidating Prowess›, ‹feat/Nerve-Racking Negotiator›, or ‹feat/Threatening Negotiator›. The butterfly blade does not need to meet the feat's prerequisites." alter="Slayer talents"}

::ab[Butterfly's Kiss (Ex)]{icon=power-boost head flavor="Butterfly blades train to inflict painful cuts rather than lethal ones." ability="A butterfly blade can deal nonlethal damage with ‹eq-weapon/butterfly sword«s› without taking a penalty on attack rolls." imp3="A butterfly blade gets a +1 bonus on attack rolls with butterfly swords, and the damage die of any butterfly sword he wields increases ‹misc/by one step›." imp9="The bonus becomes +2 and the damage die increases another step." imp15="The bonus increases to +3 and the damage die increases another step." imp6="The butterfly blade gains the *knockout* class feature, as per a ‹class/brawler› of his class level - 2." replace="Sneak attack"}

::ab[Knockout (Ex)]{next flavor="Text from the brawler class ability." icon=melee standard="Once a day, you can unleash a devastating attack that can instantly knock a target unconscious. You must announce this intent before making your attack roll. If you hit and the target takes damage from the blow, the target must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw (DC = 10 + ‹misc/half› your brawler level + the higher of your Strength or Dexterity modifier) or fall unconscious for 1d6 rounds." special="Each round on its turn, the unconscious target may attempt a new saving throw to end the effect as a @HLfull-round_action that does not provoke attacks of opportunity. Creatures immune to critical hits or nonlethal damage are immune to this ability." imp10="You can use this twice a day." imp16="You can use this three times per day."}

::ab[Innocent Butterfly (Sp)]{l=7 icon=magic ability="A butterfly blade can use ‹spell/aura of the unremarkable› once per day as a spell-like ability." imp11="He can use it twice per day." imp14="It becomes a constant spell-like ability that he can activate or deactivate as a @HLfree_action." special="The ability's caster level is equal to his slayer class level, and the DC is equal to 10 + the number of ranks the butterfly blade has in either Bluff or Intimidate, whichever is higher." replace="Stalker~swift tracker~quarry"}

::ab[Deadly Butterfly (Ex)]{l=19 icon=boost flavor="A butterfly blade becomes a bloody whirlwind in combat." passive="When he strikes a foe with a butterfly sword, he automatically confirms critical threats. In addition, any butterfly sword he wields is treated as if it has the ‹magic-enh/speed› weapon special ability. If the weapon already has the *speed* weapon special ability, this doesn't stack." replace="Improved quarry"}

cleaner.name: Cleaner
description: ## Cleaner
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/118›

A cleaner is responsible for destroying or removing incriminating evidence from a crime scene, disposing of corpses and eliminating witnesses to make a crime look like an accident or a mere disappearance.
::ab[Deceitful]{l=1 icon=power ability="A cleaner becomes skilled at deceiving others, and gains ‹feat/Deceitful› as a bonus feat." replace=Track}

::ab[Without a Trace (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power-boost ability="A cleaner is able to study a location, conceal evidence, and hide in or extricate himself quickly from that location. Studying a location takes 1 minute for each 10-foot square. Once he has studied an area, the cleaner can attempt a Disguise check to alter evidence or a Stealth check to conceal evidence (by doing things such as cleaning up bloodstains, obscuring footprints, moving a dead body, and so on). Altering an area takes 1d3 &times& 10 minutes of work per 10-foot square. Anyone attempting to determine what actually happened in that area must succeed at an opposed Perception check against the result of the cleaner's Disguise or Stealth check (cleaner's choice) or accept the altered scene as truth. The cleaner gains a +4 bonus on this check, on Sleight of Hand checks to hide objects from that location on his person, and on Stealth checks to hide within or escape from that location. This lasts as long as the scene remains unaltered (except by the cleaner)." replace="Slayer talent gained at 4th level"}

::ab[Mislead (Sp)]{l=7 icon=magic ability="A cleaner can cast ‹spell/misdirection› on himself at will. This lasts until the cleaner uses the ability again or ends the effect as a @HLstandard_action." replace=Stalker}

::list[The following slayer talents complement the cleaner archetype]{hl link=slayertalent all="foil talent~rogue talent|‹talent/camouflage›, ‹talent/fast stealth›, ‹talent/rogue crawl›, ‹talent/surprise attack›, ‹talent/terrain mastery›"}

::list[The following advanced slayer talents complement the cleaner archetype]{hl link=slayertalent all="advanced rogue talent|‹talent/knock-out blow›, ‹talent/master of disguise›~assassinate~slayer camouflage"}

covenbane.name: Covenbane
description: ## Covenbane
‹SOURCE Blood of the Coven/26›

Covenbane slayers are scarred by arcane magic at a young age, and that resonance gives them supernatural insight into the psychic bonds between others. They are adept at fighting tight-knit groups, exploiting their connections to hunt and wound teams. The churches of ‹faith/Iomedae› and ‹faith/Milani› in particular dispatch special agents to rescue hexed children from Irrisen, Razmiran, and other lands plagued by abusive arcanists and then raise them in this deadly art.

::ab[Hag Sense (Su)]{icon=boost passive="A covenbane's magical scars throb in the presence of magic. A covenbane adds half his level (minimum +1) on Perception, Sense Motive, and Survival checks to identify, recognize, or track a ‹family/hag›, arcane spellcaster, or creature with spell-like abilities." ability="He can also use his *hag sense* to recognize whether someone within 30 feet of him is using magic to hide her true form. To do this, he must succeed at a Perception check against a DC equal to 15 + the target's CR; success indicates the presence of a magical disguise, but it does not reveal the creature's true form." replace=Track order=pa}

::ab[Studied Coven (Su)]{l=2 icon=boost ability="A covenbane can designate an entire coven as his *studied target* by studying any single member. He applies his *studied target* bonus against all members of the studied creature's coven, and all members of the coven count as one target for the purposes of determining the maximum number of *studied targets* a slayer can maintain. This bonus remains even if one or more members of the coven die after the covenbane studies them." imp7="The covenbane can designate other magically linked creatures, such as those bound by a ‹template/hivemind<_swarm› or a ‹spell/telepathic bond› spell, as a coven, but he can target only three members of such a link." special="Studying a coven always requires a @HLmove_action, even if the slayer could normally study a target with a swift or immediate action." replace="Slayer talent gained at 2nd level" alter="Studied target"}

::ab[Disrupt Coven (Su)]{icon=lower l7="Anyone the slayer threatens no longer counts as an ally for the purposes of friendly spellcasting or employing teamwork feats and cannot access any special abilities gained from being part of a coven (including coven spellcasting).@FN1" replace=Stalker}

::ab[Unseen Sense (Su)]{l=11 icon=boost-power ability="The covenbane learns to use his *hag sense* to help target concealed or invisible opponents in combat. He can use his *hag sense* to detect invisible opponents as well as magically disguised creatures." special="He also gains ‹feat/Blind-Fight› as a bonus feat but gains its benefits only against spellcasters and creatures with spell-like abilities." replace="Swift tracker"}
:::fakeFootnotes
@FN[It's not explicitly stated, but since this replaces Stalker and is listed between a 2nd-level and 11th-level ability, it has been listed here as a 7th-level ability.]{from=1}

cutthroat.name: Cutthroat
description: ## Cutthroat
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/119›

Street-smart and extremely resourceful, cutthroats stalk city streets and alleyways, preying on those unfortunate enough to catch their eye. More opportunists than careful planners, they don't care who they rob or kill, and take advantage of whatever circumstances put them near a suitable target.
::cskill{gain=diplo~kno~ling lose=kd~kg~sur}

::ab[Street Stalker (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost choice="A cutthroat selects the Acrobatics, Climb, or Knowledge (local) skill. Once selected, this cannot be changed." passive="When in urban environments, the cutthroat adds 1/2 his slayer level (minimum 1) to checks attempted with this skill." order=cp replace=Track}

::ab[Opportune Target (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost flavor="A cutthroat who catches a foe by surprise can quickly study that foe to find its weaknesses." ability="If a cutthroat can act in the surprise round, once during that surprise round he can study a foe as a @HLfree_action (instead of a move action) and select it as his *studied target.*" replace="Slayer talent gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Stab and Grab (Ex)]{l=6 icon=power swift="A cutthroat can attempt a ‹rule/steal› combat maneuver against a dead or unconscious target or a *studied target* he has just confirmed a critical hit against. A dead or unconscious opponent takes a -10 penalty to its CMD against this maneuver." replace="Slayer talent gained at 6th level"}

::list[The following slayer talents complement the cutthroat archetype]{hl link=slayertalent all="poison use~rogue talent|‹talent/fast stealth›, ‹talent/surprise attack›, ‹talent/terrain mastery›~slowing strike"}

::list[The following advanced slayer talents complement the cutthroat archetype]{hl link=slayertalent all="advanced rogue talent|‹talent/knock-out blow›, ‹talent/opportunist›~assassinate~slayer camouflage"}

deliverer.name: Deliverer
description: ## Deliverer
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/119›

Also known as a divine assassin, god's blade, or wrath-bringer, a deliverer is a weapon chosen by a god to punish those who have committed an affront to that deity. Evil deities are more likely to use assassins than deliverers, but some good deities use deliverers to deal with problems of a subtler nature than a cleric, inquisitor, paladin, or warpriest can typically handle.
::prereq{other="Follower of a deity"}

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power ability="A deliverer gains proficiency with his deity's favored weapon, in addition to the slayer's normal weapon proficiencies." alter="Weapon and armor proficiency"}

::ab[Determined Zeal (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def flavor="A deliverer shrugs off attempts by his moral opposite to control or kill him." passive="On any round in which deliverer attacks an opponent whose alignment is at least two steps away from his own (such as a lawful good deliverer fighting a chaotic good or lawful evil foe, or a neutral foe with no other alignment components), he gains a +2 bonus on Will saving throws against that opponent's abilities, as well as the benefit of the ‹feat/Diehard› feat until the end of his next turn." replace="Slayer talent gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[True Believer (Ex)]{l=6 icon=def flavor="Mortal wounds cannot stop a deliverer from performing his divine duty." passive="On any round in which he attacks an opponent whose alignment is at least two steps away from his own (see Determined Zeal, above) while benefiting from the Diehard feat, he isn't staggered from having negative hit points, nor does he automatically lose 1 hit point per round." replace="Slayer talent gained at 6th level"}

::ab[Divine Anathema (Ex)]{l=10 icon=boost free="A deliverer can declare a *studied target* to be his divine anathema. The *studied target's* alignment must be at least two steps away from that of the deliverer. Against this target, the deliverer's attacks deal an additional 2d6 points of damage; this damage is directly from divine power and does not stack with the bonus damage from a holy or unholy weapon, or a weapon with a similar weapon special ability or effect." special="A deliverer can have only one divine anathema at a time." replace="Advanced slayer talent gained at 10th level"}

::list[The following slayer talents complement the deliverer archetype]{hl link=slayertalent all="foil scrutiny~rogue talent|‹talent/combat trick›, ‹talent/finesse rogue›, ‹talent/terrain mastery›, ‹talent/trap spotter›~trapfinding›"}

::list[The following advanced slayer talents complement the deliverer archetype]{hl link=slayertalent all="advanced rogue talent|‹talent/hunter's surprise›, ‹talent/master of disguise›~assassinate~nina master trick|‹ninjatrick/evasion›"}

::ab[Ex-Deliverers]{icon=warning info="A deliverer who grossly violates the code of conduct required by his deity loses all class features granted by this archetype, except for the weapon proficiencies. (He does not gain the slayer class abilities that the archetype replaces.) He cannot thereafter gain levels as a deliverer of that deity until he atones for his deeds (see the ‹spell/atonement› spell description)."}

dune_rider.name: Dune Rider
description: ## Dune Rider
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/86›
Mounted harriers, saboteurs, and skirmishers, dune riders can disrupt even the most organized opposition.

::ab[Sand Foot (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="The dune rider adds half his level as a bonus on Ride checks in warm deserts, hills, and mountains." imp11="The dune rider and his mount no longer treat sand or rocky ground as ‹rule/difficult terrain›, and they gain a +10-foot enhancement bonus to their base speed in warm deserts, hills, and mountains." replace="Track~fast tracker"}

::ab[Desert Skirmisher (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="The dune rider excels in combat in warm deserts, hills, and mountains. While in such terrain, he gains a +2 bonus on initiative, Perception, and Stealth checks, and a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls." incrementMulti="The bonus on initiative, Perception, and Stealth checks becomes +~, and the bonus on attack and damage rolls increases to +~.~0/5;4/2;2/1" replace="Studied target"}

::ab[Galloping Fire (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost passive="The penalties for the dune rider's ranged attacks while mounted decrease by 2." imp7="These penalties now decrease by 4." replace="Stalker~slayer talent gained at 4th level"}

::ab[Dust Vision (Ex)]{l=8 icon=def-boost flavor="The dune rider is at home in the desert." passive="He exists comfortably in hot conditions up to 140&deg& Fahrenheit without having to attempt Fortitude saves. His equipment is likewise protected. He takes no penalties on Perception checks from blowing sand, dust, or heat shimmers, nor do these effects grant ‹rule/concealment› to the dune rider's enemies within 30 feet. Effects that would grant total concealment work normally." replace="Slayer talent gained at 8th level"}

::ab[Dunes of Death (Su)]{l=20 icon=lower flavor="A dune rider can use the desert's heat to finish off his foes." standard="He can make a single attack at his full base attack bonus while in warm desert, hills, or mountains, choosing one of the following effects: kill, knock unconscious for 1d4 hours, or paralyze for 2d6 rounds. If the attack hits, the target takes damage normally and must succeed at a Fortitude save or suffer the additional effect. The DC of this save is 20 + the dune rider's Intelligence modifier." special="Whether or not the target succeeds, it can't be targeted by this ability again for 24 hours." replace="Master slayer"}

family_hunter.name: Family Hunter
description: ## Family Hunter
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/66›
Most slayers focus their efforts on single targets, but family hunters try to root out every branch of a tainted tree of life.

::ab[Find Family (Sp)]{l=2 icon=power choice="A family hunter chooses a particular family line of mortal creatures to cleanse (she must choose an actual family line like &quot&The Montrose family,&quot& rather than something generic that would cover all creatures of a given kind like &quot&All humans descended from the first human created by the gods&quot&)." order=ca ability="She can target any creature from that family line with ‹spell/discern next of kin› (with a caster level equal to her slayer level) at will as a spell-like ability. The save DC is Charisma-based and is modified by the slayer's *studied target* bonus if the creature is also her *studied target.* A creature that successfully saves against this effect is immune to this ability for 24 hours." special="If the family hunter's chosen family line is completely eradicated, she can choose a new one after 1 month of studying." replace="Slayer talent gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Kinslayer (Su)]{l=3 icon=boost ability="A family hunter's sneak attack deals an additional 1d6 points of damage to any creature with a blood relation to the family line she seeks to cleanse." alter="Sneak attack"}

::ab[Studied Target (Ex)]{l=5 icon=down info="A slayer can maintain more than one *studied target* at a time only if all targets are closely related to one another (even if they aren't part of the family line she seeks to cleanse). This includes blood relatives; undead spawn; constructs or undead and their creator; summoned creatures and their summoner; creatures that are charmed, dominated, or otherwise under direct magical control and their controller; and cohorts, followers, animal companions, familiars, and similar associates of one master." alter="Studied target"}

::ab[Disrupt Teamwork (Ex)]{l=6 icon=def passive="A family hunter's *studied target* never counts as an ally to its allies for the purpose of ‹rule/flanking› the family hunter or using teamwork feats or the ‹rule/aid another<_2› action against the family hunter." replace="Slayer talent gained at 6th level"}

grave_warden.name: Grave Warden
description: ## Grave Warden
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/120›

While paladins and inquisitors use their connection with the divine to fight undead hordes and other horrors of the night, a grave warden relies on knowledge, skill with weapons, and tenacity to put an end to these night-born terrors.

::ab[Holy Water Sprinkler (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power-melee ability="A grave warden can draw a flask of ‹eq-weapon/holy water› as if it were a weapon (and can thus use the ‹feat/Quick Draw› feat to draw flasks of holy water)." swift="He can open a flask of holy water and pour it onto a held or adjacent melee weapon. If the weapon successfully hits an undead creature before the end of the grave warden's next turn, the undead takes damage as if it took a direct hit from the holy water, in addition to the damage from the weapon, if any." replace="Slayer talent gained at 2nd level" order=aw}

::ab[Death Ward (Sp)]{l=7 icon=def ability="A grave warden learns to perform a short ritual that grants the benefits of ‹spell/death ward›, using his slayer level as his caster level. Performing this ritual takes 1 minute and uses 4 flasks of holy water. The grave warden can protect only himself with this ability." replace=Stalker}

::ab[Dustbringer (Ex)]{l=10 icon=boost ability="A grave warden can slay a *studied* undead opponent. This functions as the ‹slayertalent/assassinate› slayer talent, except it requires a successful Will saving throw instead of a successful Fortitude save, affects only undead, and destroys the target instead of killing it. If the target succeeds at its saving throw, it becomes immune to that grave warden's dustbringer ability for 24 hours." replace="Advanced slayer talent gained at 10th level"}
::list[The following rogue talents complement the grave warden archetype]{hl link=talent all="fast stealth~finesse rogue~surprise attack~swift poison~terrain mastery~trap spotter~unwitting ally"}
::list[The following advanced slayer talents complement the grave warden archetype]{hl link=slayertalent all="advanced rogue talent|‹talent/hunter's surprise›~slayer camouflage"}

guerrilla.name: Guerrilla
description: ## Guerrilla
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/87›
Slayers characterized by cunning and patience, guerrillas specialize in ambush and harassment of enemies.

::ab[Strike First, Strike Last (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A guerrilla adds half his level on Stealth checks when remaining motionless and reduces the penalty on Stealth checks when sniping by an equal amount. He also gains this bonus on his initiative checks in surprise rounds whenever he is aware of the surprise round and at least one enemy is unaware." replace="Track~swift tracker"}

::ab[Quick Start (Ex)]{l=6 icon=melee ability="The guerrilla can take a normal ‹rule/charge› action during the surprise round, rather than a charge with half the usual distance." replace="6th-level slayer talent"}

::ab[Astonishing Strike (Ex)]{l=14 icon=lower ability="When the guerrilla deals sneak attack damage to a target of his *studied target* ability during the surprise round, that target is denied its Dexterity bonus to AC against attacks from the same guerrilla until the end of the guerrilla's next turn." imp19="This ability works any time he deals sneak attack damage to a target of his *studied target* ability during the first round of combat, whether or not it's a surprise round." replace="Quarry~improved quarry"}

pureblade.name: Pureblade
description: ## Pureblade
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Origins/20›

First known in Numeria, these aberration hunters have spread to other regions of Golarion, even as far as the lands bordering the Mana Wastes. In Numeria, Pureblades are skilled at slaying naturally arising ‹type/aberration«s› and any creatures that have been mutated by exposure to the bizarre alien technology that pervades the land. Pureblades from Alkenstar and Nex lead forays into the magic-starved Mana Wastes to eliminate aberrations before the creatures can threaten their lands.

::ab[Aberration Hunter (Ex)]{icon=boost immediate="If a Pureblade deals damage to an aberration, he can apply his *studied target* bonus against that target even if he didn't sneak attack it." ability="In addition, a Pureblade can attempt Knowledge (dungeoneering) skill checks untrained when trying to identify aberrations." order=ia replace=Track}

::ab[Alien Prescience (Sp)]{l=2 icon=magic standard="A Pureblade can *detect aberrations.* This works like ‹spell/detect undead›, except it detects aberrations instead of undead.@FN1" replace="Slayer talent received at 2nd level"}

::ab[Discern Weakness (Ex)]{l=7 icon=roll flavor="A Pureblade becomes an expert at locating an aberration's weak points." ability="The Pureblade rolls twice to confirm critical hits against aberrations and keeps the higher roll." replace=Stalker}

::ab[Steely Mind (Ex)]{l=8 icon=def order=ps passive="A Pureblade gains a +2 resistance bonus on saving throws against mind-affecting spells and effects." increment="The resistance bonus~8~4~4~2" incrementMax=18 standard="Once per day, a Pureblade can shed a magical effect placed on him by an aberration as ‹spell/break enchantment›, using his slayer level as his caster level." imp12="A Pureblade can shed a magical effect twice per day." imp16="He can shed a magical effect three times per day." replace="Slayer talent received at 8th level"}
::ab[Aberration Slayer (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost ability="A Pureblade can use his *master slayer* ability on any aberration, even if it's not his *studied target.*" alter="Master slayer"}
:::fakeFootnotes

@FN[This archetype was published before the spell ‹spell/detect aberration› was created. It has a greater range and longer duration than *detect undead.* Consult your GM if you wish to use that spell.]{from=1}

sczarni_executioner.name: Sczarni Executioner
description: ## Sczarni Executioner
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Origins/21›

These professional killers are trained and used almost exclusively by the various Sczarni crime families. Streetwise and ruthless, they operate independently, often making their talents available to the highest bidder. Though a few unscrupulous Sczarni executioners exploit this arrangement and force families to pay higher rates for their services, most of these assassins follow a strict personal code and maintain absolute transparency with their employers.
::cskill{gain=app~diplo~ea lose=kd~kg~ride~sur}
::ab[Bloodstained Hands (Ex)]{icon=warning prereq="A Sczarni executioner must be evil." info="If he ever becomes nonevil, he loses this archetype and becomes a regular slayer."}

::ab[Focused Killer (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost-down passive="A Sczarni executioner's *studied target* bonuses and the DCs of his slayer abilities increase by 1 against humanoid opponents, but decrease by 1 against non-humanoid targets." alter="Studied target"}

::ab[Painful Strike (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power flavor="Sczarni executioners are trained to cause excruciating pain when striking targets, often leaving them reeling in agony or completely incapacitated as they slowly bleed out." ability="A Sczarni executioner automatically gains this talent. A creature that takes sneak attack damage from a Sczarni executioner must make a successful a Fortitude save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the Sczarni executioner's class level + his Intelligence modifier) or become ‹misc/sickened› for 1d4 rounds." special="This is considered a Primary Sneak Attack Talent. Only one such talent can be applied to an individual attack, and the decision of which to use must be made before the attack roll is made." alter="Slayer talent received at 4th level" replace="Swift tracker"}
::ab[Assassinate (Ex)]{l=10 icon=power ability="A Sczarni executioner must select the ‹slayertalent/assassinate› advanced slayer talent." alter="Slayer talent received at 10th level"}

::ab[Swift Death (Ex)]{l=14 icon=power ability="Once per day a Sczarni executioner can attempt to assassinate a foe without studying his foe beforehand. He must still succeed at a sneak attack against the target." imp19="He can make two such attacks per day." replace="Quarry~improved quarry"}

sniper.name: Sniper
description: ## Sniper
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/120›
Whether it's with a bullet from a sling, a quarrel from a crossbow, or even an expertly thrown dagger, the sniper ensures that a single shot disables his targets.
::ab[Accuracy (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost ability="A sniper halves all range increment penalties when making ranged attacks with a bow, crossbow, or firearm." replace=Track}

::ab[Deadly Sniper (Ex)]{l=2 icon=ranged ability="When the sniper makes an attack against a target who is within his weapon's first range increment and completely unaware of his presence, that attack ignores the 30 foot range limit on ranged sneak attacks, and if it is a sneak attack, he adds his sniper level as a bonus on his sneak attack damage roll." special="After this first attack, the target is aware of the sniper's presence."}
::list[The following rogue talents complement the sniper archetype]{hl link=talent all="camouflage~snap shot~sniper's eye~weapon training"}
::list[The following advanced rogue talents complement the sniper archetype]{hl link=talent all="deadly sneak~master of disguise~stealthy sniper"}

spawn_slayer.name: Spawn Slayer
description: ## Spawn Slayer
‹SOURCE Blood of the Ancients/24›
Generations of Ninshaburian warriors fought the Spawn of Rovagug, holding the line against impossible odds. Some of their ancient techniques persist to this day.

::ab[Studied Spawn (Ex)]{flavor="A spawn slayer specializes in fighting against larger, single targets." imp5="The bonuses on weapon attack rolls, damage rolls, and skill checks against a *studied target* increase by ‹misc/one-fifth› of his slayer level, as do the DCs of slayer abilities against a *studied target.* If the target is Large or larger, these bonuses increase by an additional 1." imp10="If the target is Huge or larger, the bonuses increase by an additional 2." imp15="If the target is Gargantuan or larger, the bonuses increase by an additional 3." imp20="If the target is Colossal in size, the bonuses increase by an additional 4." icon=boost special="A spawn slayer cannot maintain these bonuses against more than one target at a time." replace="The ability to study an opponent as a swift action at 7th level" alter="Studied target"}

::ab[Gain Leverage (Ex)]{l=7 icon=boost-def flavor="A spawn slayer learns techniques to counteract a foe's size advantage." passive="When performing a combat maneuver against a *studied target* of Large or larger size, the spawn slayer gains a bonus on the check equal to the creature's size modifier to CMB (Large +1, Huge +2, Gargantuan +4, and Colossal +8). The spawn slayer adds this same bonus to his CMD when defending against combat maneuvers from a Large or larger *studied target.*" replace=Stalker}

::ab[Force Vulnerability (Ex)]{l=20 icon=lower flavor="A spawn slayer can create an opening in even an invulnerable foe's defenses." standard="He can make a single attack against a *studied target* at his full attack bonus. If the attack hits, the damage bypasses any damage reduction and the target must succeed at a Will saving throw (DC 10 + half the slayer's level + the slayer's Intelligence modifier) or lose one of its defensive abilities for 2d6 rounds. The slayer can choose which defensive ability is lost from the following options: a single immunity, all resistances, regeneration, or spell resistance." special="Whether or not the target fails this save, it cannot be targeted by *force vulnerability* again (by you or another slayer) for 24 hours." replace="Master slayer"}

spire_diver.name: Spire Diver
description: ## Spire Diver
‹SOURCE Pathfinder #124: City in the Deep/80›

Peerless divers, assassins, and scouts, spire divers operate in and out of water using the same weapons and equipment. Although the elves of the Mordant Spire originally developed the techniques that spire divers use, people living in coastal areas and archipelagos near the Mwangi Expanse and Tian Xia have similar, independently developed warrior and diving traditions.

::ab[Studied Target (Ex)]{icon=down-boost order=na info="A spire diver does not gain the ability to add her *studied target* bonus on Bluff and Survival checks against an opponent she has studied." ability="When using any bow or crossbow against her *studied target* underwater, the spire diver ignores the normal penalty for shooting underwater and instead treats the weapon as though its range increment were 10 feet." imp7="She she cannot add her *studied target* bonus on Disguise checks." incrementPlain="The range increment~0~5~20~10" special="When using a bow or crossbow that already has an underwater range increment, such as an ‹eq-weapon/underwater crossbow›, the spire diver adds the range increment gained from *studied target* as a bonus to the weapon's usual underwater range increment, though the combined range increment cannot exceed the weapon's normal range increment." alter="Studied target~stalker"}

::ab[Hold Breath (Ex)]{icon=def ability="A spire diver increases the number of rounds she can ‹misc/hold her breath› before she is required to start attempting Constitution checks to avoid drowning by an amount equal to twice her slayer level." replace=Track}
::ab[Slayer Talents]{icon=power ability="A spire diver can select the ‹talent/strong stroke› rogue talent as a slayer talent."}
::ab[Swift Swimmer (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power ability="A spire diver gains a swim speed equal to her base speed." replace="Swift tracker"}

::ab[Diver's Advance (Ex)]{l=13 icon=power move="A spire diver can swim up to twice her swim speed once per day. She can use Stealth as part of this movement but takes a -10 penalty on her check for doing so." imp17="She can use this ability twice per day." replace="Slayer's advance"}

::ab[Diver's Quarry (Ex)]{icon=down-boost ability="A spire diver gains *quarry* and *improved quarry* as a normal slayer, but she does not gain the ability to take 10 or take 20 on Survival checks while moving at normal speed without penalty." imp14="A spire diver can take 10 on Knowledge checks to identify a monster she has selected as her *quarry.*" imp19="She can take 20 on these checks without spending extra time to do so." special="The spire diver must be trained in the appropriate Knowledge skill to use this ability." alter="Quarry~improved quarry"}

spiritslayer.name: Spiritslayer
description: ## Spiritslayer
‹SOURCE Haunted Heroes Handbook/15›

Originally trained and employed by the enforcers of the Pure Legion seeking to defend Rahadoum from divine spellcasters, Rahadoumi spiritslayers have proven so invaluable in culling ghosts, shadow demons, and other incorporeal creatures that their techniques have spread across the Inner Sea region and beyond. Ironically, most now are trained and employed by the church of Pharasma to cull wicked spirits.

::ab[Spirit Sense (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power-boost-def order=a23p flavor="A spiritslayer manifests an uncanny sixth sense regarding the presence of invisible creatures." ability="He gains ‹feat/Blind-Fight› as a bonus feat and ignores the usual +20 bonus to the DC of any Perception check to pinpoint the location of an invisible creature." ability2="If the spiritslayer manages to pinpoint an invisible creature's location using a Perception check, he can study that creature with his *studied target* ability as a @HLswift_action." ability3="In addition, whenever the spiritslayer studies a possessed creature, he can automatically switch his *studied target* from the possessed creature to the possessing entity should that entity be expelled from his *studied target.*" passive="A spiritslayer adds his *studied target* bonus on attack rolls to all saving throws he attempts against possession effects used by his *studied targets.*" replace="Slayer talent gained at 2nd level"}
::ab[Spiritslayer Talents]{head icon=power ability="A spiritslayer can select the following slayer talents, in addition to general slayer talents."}

::ab[Disrupt Possession (Ex)]{next prereq="8th-level slayer" icon=boost-protect ability="Whenever a spiritslayer with this talent deals sneak attack damage to a creature that is being possessed, he also deals his sneak attack damage to the possessing entity. Once per round, a possessed creature that is damaged by *disrupt possession* can attempt a new Will saving throw to end the possession, gaining a +1 bonus on its save for each sneak attack damage die the spiritslayer rolled." special="This talent is considered a Primary Sneak Attack Talent, alters the spiritslayer's sneak attack, and doesn't stack with other talents that alter sneak attack."}

::ab[Improved Spirit Sense]{next prereq="6th-level slayer" icon=power-boost ability="A spiritslayer who selects this talent gains ‹feat/Improved Blind-Fight› as a bonus feat, ignoring its prerequisites. In addition, he adds 1/2 his slayer level on Perception checks to pinpoint the location of an invisible creature." ability2="He can use Perception to pinpoint the location of an ethereal creature, but he doesn't gain this bonus on Perception checks when doing so." order=a2}

::ab[Greater Spirit Sense]{next prereq="10th-level slayer, Improved Spirit Sense" icon=power-boost ability="A spiritslayer who selects this talent gains ‹feat/Greater Blind-Fight› as a bonus feat, ignoring its prerequisites. In addition, he adds 1/2 his slayer level to his Perception checks to pinpoint the location of ethereal creatures, and once per round he can attempt a Perception check to pinpoint the locations of all invisible creatures within 30 feet of him without needing to take an action."}

::ab[Ghost Slayer (Ex)]{icon=lower-boost l7="Whenever a spiritslayer makes an outsider or undead creature with the ‹subtype/incorporeal› subtype his *studied target,* his attacks count as magic for the purpose of striking that creature (though this doesn't give his attacks an enhancement bonus) and he can deal precision-based damage to such creatures (though his ‹misc/precision damage› is reduced by 50% unless he has a means to bypass the creature's reduction in damage from corporeal sources, such as a ‹magic-enh/ghost touch› weapon)." l14="The spiritslayer's attacks against outsiders or undead creatures with the incorporeal subtype ignore the 50% reduction in damage from magical corporeal sources that such creatures have and can deal precision-based damage to incorporeal creatures." replace=Stalker}

stygian_slayer.name: Stygian Slayer
description: ## Stygian Slayer
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/120›

A stygian slayer crawls out of the darkest shadows to strike fear into the hearts of civilized folk. He's a merciless killer who can control a sliver of magic, allowing him to arrive unseen, commit murder, and depart without detection.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down info="A stygian slayer is proficient with light armor, but not with medium armor, heavy armor, or any kind of shield (including tower shields)." replace="Armor proficiency"}

::ab[Invisibility (Sp)]{l=4 icon=magic ability="A stygian slayer can cast ‹spell/invisibility›, using his slayer level as his caster level. The slayer uses his Intelligence modifier for ‹rule/concentration› checks when using this ability." useInc=slayer~4 useF="4~1" replace="Slayer talent gained at 4th level"}

::ab[Spell Use]{l=7 icon=magic ability="A stygian slayer is able to use spell completion and spell trigger items as if he were an arcane caster with these spells on his spell list: ‹spell/darkness›, ‹spell/forced quiet›, ‹spell/modify memory›, ‹spell/nondetection›, ‹spell/obscuring mist›, ‹spell/phantom steed›, ‹spell/shadow walk›, and wizard spells of the illusion school of spell level 0 through 4th. The slayer's uses his class level as his caster level for this ability." replace=Stalker}

::ab[Shadowy Mist Form (Sp)]{l=10 icon=magic ability="A stygian slayer can transform into an inky black cloud of mist. This functions as ‹spell/gaseous form›, except it also obscures vision as ‹spell/fog cloud›." useL=slayer useUnit=minute useNC=1 replace="Advanced slayer talent gained at 10th level"}

::list[The following slayer talents complement the stygian slayer archetype]{hl link=slayertalent all="poison use~rogue talent|‹talent/bleeding attack›, ‹talent/lasting poison›, ‹talent/powerful sneak›, ‹talent/rogue crawl›, ‹talent/slow reactions›, ‹talent/surprise attack›, ‹talent/swift poison›"}
::list[The following advanced slayer talents complement the stygian slayer archetype]{hl link=slayertalent all="advanced rogue talent|‹talent/hunter's surprise›~assassinate~slayer camouflage›"}

toxic_sniper.name: Toxic Sniper
description: ## Toxic Sniper
‹SOURCE People of the Wastes/27›

Followers of the daemonic harbinger Cixyron hide throughout the Mana Wastes, scavenging for guns. With significant firepower in hand, the Furious Thunder's snipers bring poisonous death to the wastes.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down-power ability="A toxic sniper is proficient with all simple weapons and firearms. He is proficient with light armor." alter="Weapon and armor proficiency"}

::ab[Scrapper's Gun]{icon=power ability="A toxic sniper gains the ‹feat/Gunsmithing› feat, a battered gun identical to that gained by a 1st-level ‹class/gunslinger›, and the ability to restore his battered gun as if he were a gunslinger." replace=Track}

::ab[Sharpshooter's Study (Ex)]{flavor="A toxic sniper's focus makes him extremely deadly with ranged weapons." icon=down info="He gains a bonus on attack and damage rolls against his *studied target* only when attacking with a ranged weapon." alter="Studied target"}
::ab[Toxic Grit (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="The toxic sniper gains the ‹feat/Amateur Gunslinger› feat as a bonus feat." replace="Slayer talent gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Toxic Shots (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power-boost ability="A toxic sniper can coat his ammunition in poison without requiring a ‹eq-weapon/pitted bullet›. He is trained in applying poison in this way and cannot accidentally poison himself when applying poison to his ammunition. The toxic sniper can create a number of *toxic shots* per day equal to ‹misc/half› his slayer level plus his Wisdom modifier. The penetrating nature of a *toxic shot* increases the DC of any poisons applied in this way by 2." special="A toxic sniper cannot create a *toxic shot* that is part of an ‹eq-weapon/alchemical cartridge›." replace="Slayer talent gained at 4th level"}

::ab[Marksman (Ex)]{icon=boost l6="A toxic sniper reduces the penalty on Stealth checks while sniping by 5." l8="He increases the distance from which he can deliver a ranged sneak attack against his *studied target* by 10 feet." incrementMulti="The sniping penalty is now reduced by ~.~6/6;10/5~~Ranged sneak attack distance is now increased by ~ feet.~8/6;20/10" replace="Slayer talents gained at 6th, 8th, 12th, 14th, 16th, 18th, and 20th levels"}

::ab[Precise Toxin (Ex)]{l=10 icon=boost flavor="A toxic sniper can deliver a *toxic shot* to especially vulnerable areas." ability="When he hits an opponent with a *toxic shot* and deals sneak attack damage, he can forgo all his bonus damage to increase the DC of the his *toxic shot's* poison. The DC increases by 1 for every 2d6 points of sneak attack damage he forgoes in this way." replace="Slayer talent gained at 10th level"}

turncoat.name: Turncoat
description: ## Turncoat
‹SOURCE Heroes of the Darklands/17›

In Sekamina, betraying others before they have the chance to betray you can be a necessary survival technique. While not all adventurers have the heart for such underhanded dealings, others have developed a knack for it. The turncoat's natural talents for deception and treachery are fitting when negotiating with the untrustworthy inhabitants of Sekamina and other notoriously unscrupulous areas, and his ability to adapt allows him to preempt opponents' schemes. Not all party members may be comfortable with having someone so adept at backstabbing among their ranks, but most tend to be grateful that he uses his skills to their benefit.

::ab[Dubious Recognition (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A turncoat adds half his level (minimum 1) to Sense Motive checks to recognize when a creature is lying or attempting to take advantage of the turncoat and his allies." replace=Track}

::ab[Double Speak (Ex)]{l=7 icon=boost ability="A turncoat can attempt a Bluff check in place of a Diplomacy check against a *studied target.* The target can attempt an opposed Sense Motive check." replace=Stalker}

::ab[Sudden Betrayal (Ex)]{l=14 icon=boost standard="A turncoat can choose a single creature within 30 feet. He gains a +4 circumstance bonus on ‹skill/Diplomacy› checks against this target. If the target has an attitude of indifferent or better toward him, the first attack roll the turncoat makes against that target is automatically treated as threatening a critical hit. If the target's attitude is friendly or better, a successful attack is treated as a confirmed critical hit. This ability can be used only once per day." imp19="The bonus increases to +6, and the attack is treated as a confirmed critical hit if the target is at least indifferent to the turncoat." replace="Quarry~improved quarry"}

vanguard.name: Vanguard
description: ## Vanguard
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/121›

Vanguards are battlefield commanders who focus on the brutality of combat and lead their allies to bloody victory. Quick to react to danger, a vanguard is a valuable scout, capable officer, and skilled tactician.
::ab[Lookout (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="A vanguard adds 1/2 his level (minimum 1) to initiative checks." replace=Track}

::ab[Tactician (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power order=as ability="A vanguard receives a ‹main/teamwork feat› as a bonus feat. He must meet the prerequisites for this feat." standard="Once per day, the vanguard can grant one of his teamwork feats to all allies within 30 feet who can see and hear him. Allies retain the use of this bonus feat for 3 rounds plus 1 round for every 2 levels the vanguard possesses. Allies do not need to meet the prerequisites of this bonus feat." special="Whenever the vanguard is able to select a new slayer talent, he can instead choose to gain an additional use per day of this ability." replace="Slayer talent gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Vanguard's Bond (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost flavor="A vanguard forms a bond with his fighting companions." move="He can choose one of his current *studied targets* and grant ‹misc/half› his *studied target* bonus against that target to all allies within 30 feet who can see or hear him. This bonus lasts for a number of rounds equal to the slayer's Intelligence modifier (minimum 1)." special="This bonus does not stack with any ‹ability/favored enemy› or *studied target* bonuses possessed by his allies; they use whichever bonus is higher." replace="Slayer talent gained at 4th level"}

::ab[Ever Ready (Ex)]{l=7 icon=boost passive="A vanguard is never considered an ‹rule/unaware combatant»<surprise› and is always able to act in the surprise round. The vanguard is still ‹rule/flat-footed› in the surprise round until he acts." replace=Stalker}
::list[The following rogue talents complement the vanguard archetype]{hl link=talent all="combat trick~finesse rogue~hard to fool~rogue crawl~surprise attack~terrain mastery~weapon training"}

::list[The following advanced slayer talents complement the vanguard archetype]{hl link=slayertalent all="advanced rogue talent|‹talent/feat›, ‹talent/opportunist›, ‹talent/unwitting ally›~slayer camouflage"}

velvet_blade.name: Velvet Blade
description: ## Velvet Blade
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/70›

Velvet blades specialize in infiltrating the upper echelons of society, mingling with nobles and courtiers, and killing them. Velvet blades are often born into the aristocracy, with an intricate understanding of the inner workings of upper-class society. Though a few velvet blades are unhinged maniacs who enjoy preying on their peers, most of these well-heeled slayers assume a professional demeanor and hire themselves out to other nobles or well-connected criminal organizations.
::cskill{gain=diplo~kno lose=kd}
::ab[Armor Proficiency]{icon=down info="A velvet blade is not proficient with medium armor or shields." alter="Armor proficiency"}

::ab[Studied Socialite (Ex)]{flavor="A velvet blade focuses her studies on social infiltration rather than brute force, believing it is easier to kill an opponent quietly and unprepared than in a loud battle." passive="She adds her *studied target* bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, Disguise, Intimidate, Knowledge, Perception, Sense Motive, Stealth, and Survival checks against *studied* opponents, as well as to the DCs of slayer class abilities she uses against such opponents." special="She never gains a bonus on weapon attack and damage rolls from her *studied target* ability." icon=boost-down alter="Studied target"}

::ab[Courtly Graces (Ex)]{flavor="A velvet blade spends long hours memorizing etiquette and family lineages of royalty and other prominent families." icon=boost passive="She gains a bonus on Knowledge (nobility) checks equal to ‹misc/half› her slayer level (minimum +1)." replace=Track}

::ab[Treacherous Blade (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power-boost flavor="A velvet blade can capitalize on how others underestimate her and allow her close to them, assuming she is harmless." ability="She gains the ‹feat/Betrayer› feat as a bonus feat, whether or not she meets the prerequisites. Additionally, when making her first attack against a *studied target* that believes her to be harmless or to be an ally, the attack counts as a sneak attack, even if the target isn't flanked or denied its Dexterity bonus to AC, and the velvet blade gains +2d6 sneak attack damage." increment="The sneak attack damage~5~5~3" incrementEnd="d6." special="Even if she immediately attacks the target again (such as by continuing a full attack), only the first attack gains these benefits, since the target immediately realizes the velvet blade is something more than she seemed."}

::ab[Silent Dispatch (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power flavor="A velvet blade becomes adept at taking out her targets quietly." ability="She gains the ‹vigtalent/silent dispatch› vigilante talent without meeting the talent's prerequisites." replace="Swift tracker"}

witch_killer.name: Witch Killer
description: ## Witch Killer
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/67›
Born into cultures where magic users are hated and feared, witch killers devote their lives to purge the arcane taint from the world.
::cskill{gain=ka~spc}

::ab[Studied Witch (Ex)]{icon=boost-down ability="A witch killer's *studied combat* bonus applies on Spellcraft checks and saving throws against arcane spells and spell-like abilities and hexes. This bonus is reduced by 1 (minimum 0) if the target is not an arcane spellcaster, or a being called or summoned by arcane magic." special="He does not gain the increases to the maximum number of *studied targets* he can have at a time normally attained at 5th, 10th, 15th, and 20th levels, but his bonuses still increase at those levels." alter="Studied target"}

::ab[Superstitous (Ex)]{icon=power ability="Instead of being able to select ranger combat style feats as slayer talents, a witch killer can select any of the following barbarian rage powers as slayer talents, treating his slayer level as his barbarian level: ‹ragepower/clear mind›, ‹ragepower/disruptive›, ‹ragepower/eater of magic›, ‹ragepower/spell sunder›, ‹ragepower/spellbreaker›, ‹ragepower/sunder enchantment›, ‹ragepower/superstition›, or ‹ragepower/witch hunter›." special="If the witch killer selects a rage power that can normally be used only once per rage (*eater of magic, spell sunder,* or *sunder enchantment*), he can use it once per day per 5 slayer levels he possesses, though no more than once per round in any event. The other rage powers affect the slayer constantly, though the *superstition* and *witch hunter* rage powers apply only against arcane spells and spell-like abilities and the creatures that use them."}

::ab[Lingering Thrust (Ex)]{l=4 icon=lower order=a2 ability="When a witch killer's attack against an arcane spellcaster in the midst of casting forces the spellcaster to attempt a ‹rule/concentration› check, the witch killer adds half his slayer level to the check's DC." ability2="When he makes a sneak attack against an arcane spellcaster, up to 1 point of sneak attack damage per slayer level counts as ongoing damage for the purpose of forcing the spellcaster to attempt concentration checks (the witch killer does not add half his slayer level to this DC)." replace="Slayer talent gained at 4th level"}

::ab[Scent Magic (Su)]{l=5 icon=magic order=ap flavor="A witch killer can detect arcane magic by scent." ability="He can use ‹spell/detect magic› at will (as per a ‹class/paladin«'s› ability to *detect evil*), but only against arcane magic, and the ability is scent-based and thus defeated by effects like ‹spell/negate aroma›." passive="Even when he isn't concentrating on using his *magic scent,* when a creature within scent range casts an arcane spell (including arcane spell-like abilities or magic item effects), the witch killer automatically pinpoints the caster's location."}

::ab[Burn the Witch (Sp)]{l=10 icon=magic ability="Once per day when a witch killer observes a creature within 30 feet cast an arcane spell or use an arcane spell-like ability, he can use ‹spell/foster hatred› on that character, or target one creature harmed by the arcane effect with ‹spell/vengeful outrage›, identifying the caster as the enemy." incrementPlain="He can use this~10~5" incrementDesc=ability incrementEnd=" times a day." special="The save DC is Charisma-based and uses the witch killer's slayer level as his caster level." replace="Slayer talent gained at 10th level"}

woodland_sniper.name: Woodland Sniper
description: ## Woodland Sniper
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/87›
Woodland snipers are guardians of forest and grove, keeping vigil on their borders and hunting bounties and trespassers from the shelter of sturdy branches.

::ab[Tree Climber (Ex)]{icon=boost-ranged order=pa passive="A woodland sniper adds half his level to Acrobatics and Climb checks to move between, through, or up trees." ability="With a successful DC 15 Acrobatics check, he can make ranged attacks with bows while balancing on branches." replace=Track}

::ab[Ranged Sneak Attack (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost ability="Whenever a woodland sniper makes a ranged attack against a creature within 30 feet that is denied its Dexterity bonus to AC (whether the target actually has a Dexterity bonus or not), he deals additional damage. This damage is a number of d6 equal to ‹misc/one-third› of his slayer level. Should the woodland sniper score a critical hit with his ranged attack, this additional damage is not multiplied." special="A woodland sniper cannot use sneak attack with a melee attack." imp6="The range at which he can make a sneak attack increases by 10 feet." incrementPlain="The range at which he can make a sneak attack is now increased~6~3~20~10" incrementDesc=by incrementEnd=" feet." alter="Sneak attack"}

::ab[Still Shot (Ex)]{l=7 icon=boost passive="When he has ‹rule/cover› provided by one or more trees, the woodland sniper reduces the penalty on Stealth checks to remain hidden while sniping by half his slayer level." replace=Stalker}

::ab[Branchwalking (Ex)]{icon=power l11="The woodland sniper gains a climb speed equal to his base speed." l13="He can move from one tree to another via connecting branches by adding 5 to the DC of an Acrobatics or Climb check to move within the canopy. The woodland sniper cannot end his movement on a branch that cannot support his weight." replace="Swift tracker~slayer's advance"}
